Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Territorial Lands Act
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Territorial Lands Act

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Territorial Lands Act to authorize the Governor in Council to make regulations for the acquisition of mining rights in, under or on territorial lands and to require that any such regulations must provide for the free, prior and informed consent of Inuit and of any other Indigenous peoples who would be directly affected.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Section 12 of the Territorial Lands Act is replaced by the following:

The Governor in Council may make regulations for the acquisition of mining rights in, under or on territorial lands and the payment of royalties in respect of the acquisition, but such regulations shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provide that any such acquisition requires the free, prior and informed consent of Inuit and of any other Indigenous peoples who would be directly affected by it; and
provide for the protection of and compensation to the holders of surface rights.
